title: Arakkus

{{Quote|His type specializes in ''destruction'', kid! Anything ''worthwhile'' is out of his line!|Han Solo|<ref name="Deadly Reunion" />}}
[[Doctor/Legends|Dr.]] '''Arakkus''' was a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] and director of an Imperial weapons development complex during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Arakkus was severely irradiated in a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] attack that destroyed his facility, leaving him dependent on a protective suit to fend off his impending [[Death/Legends|death]]. Embittered, Arakkus used [[negatron]] charges to accelerate the collapse of a [[Collapsed dwarf star|dwarf star]], using its intense [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] pull to lure many passing [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to their doom. Rebel leaders [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] eventually overpowered and defeated Arakkus, condemning him to die in his [[Graveyard of Lost Ships]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|For all purposes, I am doomed. Therefore I've devoted my final experiment to dooming others—particularly Rebels like you!|Arakkus|<ref name="Deadly Reunion" />}}
[[Doctor/Legends|Dr.]] Arakkus was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] and director of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] weapons development complex. When his lab was destroyed by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], one of Arakkus' [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] experiments backfired upon him, heavily irradiating him and confining him to a protective suit that only slowed his eventual [[Death/Legends|demise]]. His fate sealed, the vengeful Arakkus withdrew to an area near the [[Kabal system/Legends|Kabal system]] to take part in his final experiment. Arakkus created a new home inside abandoned [[Imperial Action transport|Imperial transport]], and used a [[negatron]] impact charge bombardment to accelerate the collapse of a [[Collapsed dwarf star|dwarf star]], making it so dense its [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] pull was impossible to overcome.<ref name="Deadly Reunion" />

Blaming the Rebels for his impending death, Arakkus lured numerous Rebel ships to their doom, eventually creating a [[Graveyard of Lost Ships]]. Arakkus' experiment finally came to an end when his [[Star/Legends|star]] ensnared the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'', a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] crewed by Rebel leaders [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Solo and Skywalker managed to overpower Arakkus and steal the negatron detonator he had strapped to his suit, and while the Rebels intended to take him alive, Arakkus was determined to die where he stood. Solo and Skywalker granted his wish, detonating the entire ship graveyard behind them as they escaped on the ''Millennium Falcon''.<ref name="Deadly Reunion" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|A philosopher as well as a Rebel sympathizer?! Having you perish with me is a pleasure, Captain!|Arakkus, to Han Solo|<ref name="Deadly Reunion" />}}
Embittered by his irradiation and the knowledge of his impending death, Arakkus directed his rage outward, developing a desire to bring others to the grave with him—particularly the Rebels who were responsible for his fate. Arakkus became committed to die in his new home in the Graveyard of Lost Ships, and chose a violent death aboard his ship over a continued existence in a Rebel [[Prison/Legends|prison]]. A male Human, Arakkus had [[Color/Legends|gray]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and light skin, which was covered in green splotches after being contaminated by radiation. In addition, his [[Eye/Legends|eyes']] pupils became black while his sclerae turned orange.<ref name="Deadly Reunion" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|This protective suit ''slows'' its effects…but for all purposes, I am ''doomed''.|Arakkus|<ref name="Deadly Reunion" />}}
To slow the effects of his contamination, Arrakus wore a gold and red protective suit, attached to which was a device that would automatically detonate his Imperial transport's cargo of [[negatron]] impact charges should anything happen to him. He was also protected by a pair of [[guard-globe seeker droid]]s.<ref name="Deadly Reunion" />

==Behind the scenes==
Arakkus was originally created for the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars (comic strip)|newspaper comic strip]] ''[[Deadly Reunion]]'', written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and penciled by [[Al Williamson]], which ran from [[November 2]], [[1981]] to [[January 3]], [[1982]].<ref name="Deadly Reunion" />
